text.title: Asset Harvest Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every asset-harvest-checklist artefact must obey. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Checklist MUST cover all five categories: visual, textual, legal, analytics, seo. Missing any category invalidates the checklist.
rule.rationale: Each category is a class of error that has caused a real post-launch fix in the audited projects; partial checklists miss one whole class.

rule.statement: Every item MUST carry a source field (URL / file path / generator script). Items with source='TBD' or empty are invalid.
rule.rationale: Without a source, ownership is ambiguous; assets get re-created last-minute under stress.

rule.statement: Launch MUST be blocked if any item with required_by_launch=true is in status=missing. Optional items can ship missing with a follow-up task.
rule.rationale: Without the gate, the checklist becomes documentation rather than enforcement; required items slip.

rule.statement: Every public page MUST carry og:image (1200x630), og:title, og:description, AND favicon (multiple sizes). These items MUST be required_by_launch=true.

rationale.source: Open Graph protocol + Internal — social-share audit

rule.rationale: First-impression on Twitter/LinkedIn/Slack uses these; blanks dominate the perception of the page.

rule.statement: If analytics tracking is enabled, a consent gate (cookie banner OR analytics-without-cookies) MUST be present and tracked as a launch-required item.
rule.rationale: Analytics without consent is a regulatory risk; both must ship together or neither.
